member.name: T:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.BuildPipelineTasks.BuildLayoutGenerationTask
member.summary: The BuildTask used to generate the bundle layout.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.BuildPipelineTasks.BuildLayoutGenerationTask.Version
member.summary: The GenerateLocationListsTask version.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.BuildPipelineTasks.BuildLayoutGenerationTask.BundleNameRemap
member.summary: The mapping of the old to new bundle names. Instead of using this directly inject the value through IBuildLayoutParUse BuildLayoutParameters.BundleNameRemap instead

Calculates the Efficiency of bundle and all bundles below it in the dependency tree and caches the results. Example: There are 3 bundles A, B, and C, that are each 10 MB on disk. A depends on 2 MB worth of assets in B, and B depends on 4 MB worth of assets in C. The Efficiency of the dependencyLink from A->B would be 2/10 -> 20% and the ExpandedEfficiency of A->B would be (2 + 4)/(10 + 10) -> 6/20 -> 30%
member.param: the root of the dependency tree that the CalculateEfficiency call will start from.
Cache of all bundle dependencies that have already been calculated

member.summary: Represents a dependency from the containing Bundle to dependentBundle, with AssetDependencies representing each of the assets in parentBundle that create the link to dependentBundle

member.name: F:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.Layout.BuildLayout.Bundle.BundleDependency.DependencyBundle
member.summary: The bundle that the parent bundle depends on

member.name: F:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.Layout.BuildLayout.Bundle.BundleDependency.AssetDependencies
member.summary: The list of assets that link the parent bundle to the DependencyBundle

member.name: F:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.Layout.BuildLayout.Bundle.BundleDependency.ExpandedEfficiency
member.summary: Percentage of Efficiency asset usage that uses the entire dependency tree of this bundle dependency. This includes DependencyBundle and all bundles beneath it. Value is equal to [Total Filesize of Dependency Assets] / [Total size of all dependency bundles on disk] Example: There are 3 bundles A, B, and C, that are each 10 MB on disk. A depends on 2 MB worth of assets in B, and B depends on 4 MB worth of assets in C. The Efficiency of the dependencyLink from A->B would be 2/10 -> 20% and the ExpandedEfficiency of A->B would be (2 + 4)/(10 + 10) -> 6/20 -> 30%

member.name: F:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.Layout.BuildLayout.Bundle.BundleDependency.Efficiency
member.summary: The Efficiency of the connection between the parent bundle and DependencyBundle irrespective of the full dependency tree below DependencyBundle. Value is equal to [Serialized Filesize of assets In Dependency Bundle Referenced By Parent]/[Total size of Dependency Bundle on disk] Example: Given two Bundles A and B that are each 10 MB on disk, and A depends on 5 MB worth of assets in B, then the Efficiency of DependencyLink A->B is 5/10 = .5

member.name: F:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Build.Layout.BuildLayout.Bundle.BundleDependency.referencedAssetsFileSize
member.summary: The number of uniquely assets that the parent bundle uniquely references in dependency bundle. This is used to calculate Efficiency without double counting.

member.summary: Represents a dependency from a root Asset to a dependent Asset.

member.summary: The root Asset.

Extra data to append to catalog entries.
Map of guids to AssetLoadInfo for object identifiers in an Asset. If null, ContentBuildInterface gathers object ids automatically.
Any unknown provider types are added to this set in order to ensure they are not stripped.
Flag indicating if address locations should be included
Flag indicating if guid locations should be included
Flag indicating if label locations should be included
The internal ids of the asset, typically shortened versions of the asset's GUID.

member.summary: A flag indicating whether or not a compatible version of the CCD package is installed for use with the CCD integration workflow

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.SimulatedLoadDelay
member.summary: The load delay to be added to asset loads when Use Asset Database (fastest) is enabled. Default value is 0.1f.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.CatalogRequestsTimeout
member.summary: The maximum time to download hash and json catalog files before a timeout error.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.MaxConcurrentWebRequests
member.summary: The maximum number of concurrent web requests. This value will be clamped from 1 to 1024.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.UseUnityWebRequestForLocalBundles
member.summary: If true, local asset bundles will be loaded through UnityWebRequest.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BundleTimeout
member.summary: Attempt to abort after the number of seconds in timeout have passed, where the bundle UnityWebRequest has received no data.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BundleRetryCount
member.summary: Indicates the number of times the bundle request will be retried.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BundleRedirectLimit
member.summary: Indicates the number of redirects which this bundle UnityWebRequest will follow before halting with a “Redirect Limit Exceeded” system error.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.UniqueBundleIds
member.summary: Set this to true to ensure unique bundle ids. Set to false to allow duplicate bundle ids.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.EnableJsonCatalog
member.summary: Set this true to use Json catalogs instead of Binary catalogs. Set to false to use binary catalogs.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.ContiguousBundles
member.summary: If set, packs assets in bundles contiguously based on the ordering of the source asset which results in improved asset loading times. Disable this if you've built bundles with a version of Addressables older than 1.12.1 and you want to minimize bundle changes.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.NonRecursiveBuilding
member.summary: If set, Calculates and build asset bundles using Non-Recursive Dependency calculation methods. This approach helps reduce asset bundle rebuilds and runtime memory consumption.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.OptimizeCatalogSize
member.summary: Enables size optimization of content catalogs. This may increase the cpu usage of loading the catalog.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BuildRemoteCatalog
member.summary: Determine if a remote catalog should be built-for and loaded-by the app.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BundleLocalCatalog
member.summary: Whether the local catalog should be serialized in an asset bundle or as json.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.DisableCatalogUpdateOnStartup
member.summary: Tells Addressables if it should check for a Content Catalog Update during the initialization step.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.InternalIdNamingMode
member.summary: Internal Id mode for assets in bundles.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.InternalBundleIdMode
member.summary: Internal bundle naming mode

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.AssetLoadMode
member.summary: Will load all Assets into memory from the AssetBundle after the AssetBundle is loaded.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BundledAssetProviderType
member.summary: The provider type to use for loading assets from bundles.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.AssetBundleProviderType
member.summary: The provider type to use for loading asset bundles.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.StripUnityVersionFromBundleBuild
member.summary: If true, this option will strip the Unity Editor Version from the header of the AssetBundle during a build.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.DisableVisibleSubAssetRepresentations
member.summary: If true, the build will assume that sub Assets have no visible asset representations (are not visible in the Project view) which results in improved build times. However sub assets in the built bundles cannot be accessed by AssetBundle.LoadAsset<T> or AssetBundle.LoadAllAssets<T>.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.IgnoreUnsupportedFilesInBuild
member.summary: Whether unsupported files during build should be ignored or treated as an error.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.SharedBundleSettings
member.summary: Determines the group whose settings used for shared bundles (Built In and MonoScript bundles).

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.SharedBundleSettingsCustomGroupIndex
member.summary: Indentifies the group whose settings are used for shared bundles (Built In and MonoScript bundles).

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.GetSharedBundleGroup
member.summary: Retrieves the group whose settings are used for shared bundles (Built In and MonoScript bundles).
member.returns: The group whose settings are used.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BuiltInBundleNaming
member.summary: Sets the naming convention used for the Unity built in bundle at build time. The recommended setting is Project Name.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BuiltInBundleCustomNaming
member.summary: Custom Unity built in bundle prefix that is used if AddressableAssetSettings.BuiltInBundleNaming is set to BuiltInBundleNaming.Custom.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.MonoScriptBundleNaming
member.summary: Sets the naming convention used for the MonoScript bundle at build time. Or disabled MonoScript bundle generation. The recommended setting is Project Name.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.CheckForContentUpdateRestrictionsOption
member.summary: Informs the Addressable system how to handle checking for Content Update Restrictions during a Content Update build. During this check, assets are flagged that have changed, yet are contained in a Group that has the Cannot Change Post Release option set.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.AllowNestedBundleFolders
member.summary: If true, allows nested folders in the bundle output folder. This is useful for organizing bundles into subfolders. This enables legacy behavior.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.MonoScriptBundleCustomNaming
member.summary: Custom MonoScript bundle prefix that is used if AddressableAssetSettings.MonoScriptBundleNaming is set to MonoScriptBundleNaming.Custom.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.RemoteCatalogBuildPath
member.summary: The path to place a copy of the content catalog for online retrieval. To do any content updates to an existing built app, there must be a remote catalog. Overwriting the catalog is how the app gets informed of the updated content.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.RemoteCatalogLoadPath
member.summary: The path to load the remote content catalog from. This is the location the app will check to look for updated catalogs, which is the only indication the app has for updated content.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.ContentStateBuildPath
member.summary: The path used for saving the addressables_content_state.bin file. If empty, this will be the addressable settings config folder in your project.

member.name: P:UnityEditor.AddressableAssets.Settings.AddressableAssetSettings.BuildAddressablesWithPlayerBuild
member.summary: Defines if Addressables content will be built along with a Player build. (Requires 2021.2 or above)
member.remarks: Build with Player, will build Addressables with a Player build, this overrides preferences value. Do not Build with Player, will not build Addressables with a Player build, this overrides preferences value. Preferences value, will build with the Player dependant on is the user preferences value for "Build Addressables on Player build" is set.
